content:
How to Apply to Boston City Campus & Business College

Thinking about studying at Boston City Campus & Business College? Based in Multiple campuses, Nationwide, Boston City Campus is a Private Higher Education Institution established in 1983. This guide covers everything you need to know to apply successfully — from APS requirements to application deadlines, bursaries, and step-by-step instructions.

At a Glance: Boston City Campus Fast Facts

DetailInformation
Full NameBoston City Campus & Business College
TypePrivate Higher Education Institution
LocationCampuses across South Africa and online
Year Founded1983
Application Portalhttps://www.boston.ac.za/how-to-enrol/
Application FeeNo application fee
Applications OpenYear-round
Applications CloseRolling admissions
Minimum APS16 (varies by programme)
Contact Phone0860 267 866
Contact Emailinfo@boston.ac.za

Why Choose Boston City Campus?

One of SA's oldest private colleges, founded in 1983.

  • One of South Africa's first private higher education institutions, founded in 1983
  • Part of the Boston Media House group
  • Offers some of the most affordable private education in SA
  • Strong in distance/online learning options for working students

Faculties and Programmes at Boston City Campus

Boston City Campus offers qualifications across 4 faculties:

  • Business
  • Computing & IT
  • Humanities
  • Law

Popular programmes include: Higher Certificate in Business Management, Higher Certificate in IT, Diploma: Human Resources, Higher Certificate in Bookkeeping.

APS Requirements for Boston City Campus

The minimum Admission Point Score (APS) required at Boston City Campus is 16 points. However, individual programmes may require significantly higher scores:

APS range: 16–22 depending on programme

Boston has very accessible entry requirements, starting at APS 16 for Higher Certificates. Ideal for students who did not qualify for university but want to continue their education and improve their career prospects.

How to Calculate Your APS

Your APS is calculated from your Grade 12 (NSC) subject results. Each subject receives a score from 1 to 7 based on your percentage:

Percentage (%)Rating CodeAPS Points
80–100%7 (Outstanding)7
70–79%6 (Meritorious)6
60–69%5 (Substantial)5
50–59%4 (Adequate)4
40–49%3 (Moderate)3
30–39%2 (Elementary)2
0–29%1 (Not Achieved)1

Your APS is the sum of your six best subjects (excluding Life Orientation, which is counted at half-value at most universities, or excluded entirely).

Use our free APS Calculator to calculate your score instantly.

Step-by-Step: How to Apply to Boston City Campus

Step 1: Check Programme Requirements

Before applying, confirm the specific APS requirements and subject prerequisites for your chosen programme at https://www.boston.ac.za. Some programmes require specific subjects (e.g., Mathematics for Engineering, Life Sciences for Medicine).

Step 2: Gather Your Documents

You will typically need:

  • Certified copy of your ID (South African ID or passport for international students)
  • Certified copy of your Grade 11 final results (if applying before matric)
  • Certified copy of your NSC / Grade 12 results (final matric results when available)
  • Proof of residence (e.g., utility bill)
  • Completed application form (online or paper)

Step 3: Apply Online

Visit the Boston City Campus online application portal at https://www.boston.ac.za/how-to-enrol/. Create an account, complete the application form, upload your documents, and pay the application fee of No application fee.

Step 4: Submit Before the Deadline

Applications for 2027 admission typically open in Year-round and close on Rolling admissions. Apply as early as possible — popular programmes fill up quickly, especially Medicine, Law, and Engineering.

Step 5: Accept Your Offer

Once you receive a conditional or unconditional offer from Boston City Campus, accept it within the specified timeframe (usually 2–4 weeks). You may need to pay a registration deposit to secure your place.

Step 6: Register for Your Programme

After accepting your offer, complete the formal registration process in January (or mid-year for Semester 2 programmes). Bring original and certified copies of all documents.

Application Deadlines for Boston City Campus

Missing the application deadline is one of the most common reasons students don't get into their chosen institution. Here are the key dates:

  • Applications Open: Year-round
  • Application Deadline: Rolling admissions
  • Results Notification: Typically November–December for the following year
  • Registration: January (Semester 1) or mid-year

Pro tip: Don't wait until the last day. Apply when applications open — some programmes are filled on a first-come, first-served basis.

Bursaries and Financial Aid at Boston City Campus

Studying at Boston City Campus can be financed through several bursaries and financial aid options:

  • No NSFAS
  • Boston Scholarship Programme
  • Student loans via banks
  • Employer-sponsored bursaries for working students

Note: As a private institution, Boston City Campus does not qualify for NSFAS funding. However, many private institutions offer their own bursaries, and bank student loans (Nedbank, FNB, Absa, Standard Bank) are widely available.

Preparing for Boston City Campus While Still in School

The best time to prepare for university is while you're still in Grade 10, 11, or 12. Here's what you can do:

  • Focus on your key subjects: Make sure you're strong in the subjects required for your chosen programme
  • Use AI tutoring: StudyBuddy's AI tutor helps you master difficult CAPS topics before matric
  • Research your options early: Visit open days at Boston City Campus to understand campus life
  • Apply for bursaries early: Some bursaries (like Allan Gray, Anglo American) are available while you're still in Grade 11
  • Track your APS: Use our APS calculator to know where you stand

What Happens After You Apply?

After submitting your application to Boston City Campus, the process typically follows these stages:

  1. Application Confirmation: You'll receive an email or SMS confirming receipt of your application
  2. Conditional Offer: If your Grade 11 results meet the requirements, you may receive a conditional offer
  3. Final Results Submission: Submit your final NSC results in January
  4. Unconditional Offer: If your matric results meet or exceed the requirements, your offer becomes unconditional
  5. Registration: Register for your programme and pay your fees or confirm NSFAS/bursary funding

Comparing Boston City Campus With Other Universities

Not sure if Boston City Campus is the right fit? Here are some things to consider:

FactorBoston City CampusTypical Alternative
TypePrivate Higher Education InstitutionTraditional University / UoT
LocationMultiple campusesVaries
Minimum APS1620–37 (varies widely)
Application FeeNo application feeR100–R400
NSFAS EligibleNoYes (public) / No (private)

Frequently Asked Questions About Applying to Boston City Campus

Still have questions? Our FAQ section below answers the most common questions about applying to Boston City Campus.

Need extra help with your Grade 12 subjects to meet Boston City Campus's entry requirements? StudyBuddy's AI tutor is FREE to start — get personalised help in Mathematics, Sciences, English, and all CAPS subjects. Most students improve their marks by 20–30% within a few weeks.

quickAnswer: To apply to Boston City Campus & Business College, visit https://www.boston.ac.za/how-to-enrol/, pay the No application fee application fee, and submit your documents before Rolling admissions. Minimum APS is 16 for most programmes.

faqs.question: What is the minimum APS to apply to Boston City Campus?
faqs.answer: The minimum APS at Boston City Campus is 16 points for most programmes. However, competitive programmes like Medicine, Law, or Engineering may require significantly higher scores (16–22 depending on programme). Always check the specific requirements for your chosen programme on the Boston City Campus website.

faqs.question: When do applications open for Boston City Campus?
faqs.answer: Applications for Boston City Campus typically open in Year-round and close on Rolling admissions. We strongly recommend applying as early as possible, as popular programmes fill up quickly. Visit https://www.boston.ac.za/how-to-enrol/ to apply online.

faqs.question: How much is the application fee for Boston City Campus?
faqs.answer: The application fee at Boston City Campus is No application fee. Some students may qualify for a fee waiver — check the Boston City Campus website for details. The application fee is usually non-refundable even if your application is unsuccessful.

faqs.question: Does Boston City Campus accept NSFAS?
faqs.answer: No, Boston City Campus is a private institution and does not qualify for NSFAS funding. However, the institution offers its own financial aid, and student loans from banks (Nedbank, FNB, Absa) are available. Some bursaries from private sector organisations may also apply.

faqs.question: What documents do I need to apply to Boston City Campus?
faqs.answer: For your application to Boston City Campus, you will need: a certified copy of your ID, certified copies of your Grade 11 and/or Grade 12 results, proof of residence, and any programme-specific requirements (portfolio for arts, HPAT scores for health sciences, etc.). All documents should be certified within 3 months of the application date.
